items.title: Positive Breakout: These 12 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
items.description: In the Nifty500 pack, 12 stocks' closing prices crossed above their 200 DMA (Daily Moving Averages) on February 16, 2026, according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. The 200-day daily moving average (DMA) is used by traders as a key indicator for determining the overall trend in a particular stock. As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily timeframe, it is generally considered to be in an overall uptrend. Take a look:

items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: IGL, Siemens Energy among 5 stocks that could give 10-40% return
items.description: Brokerages remain constructive on select large- and mid-cap names, highlighting meaningful upside potential despite recent market volatility.We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources:

items.title: AI scare’s $56 billion hit tests resilience of India’s IT stocks
items.description: Indian tech stocks, including TCS and Infosys, have seen a significant market value drop due to AI fears. However, analysts and investors believe this selloff presents a buying opportunity. They argue that Indian IT firms are well-positioned to adapt, reskill, and capitalize on the growing demand for AI integration services, citing their history of technological resilience and strong financial positions.

items.title: GIFT Nifty down 70 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: Indian benchmarks ended their losing streak on Monday, driven by banking stocks. Analysts anticipate a sideways market with a slight positive bias, influenced by Infosys' AI investor meet and the India AI Impact Summit, which could guide tech stocks. The Nifty's movement around 25,600 will be key for the near-term uptrend.

items.title: Stocks in news: Fractal Analytics, RIL, Cochin Shipyard, Trent, Britannia
items.description: Markets saw volatility but recovered modestly on Monday, with analysts maintaining a cautious outlook. Several stocks like Fractal Analytics, RIL, and Cochin Shipyard are in focus due to significant news, including joint ventures, expansion plans, and major order wins. The upcoming weekly expiry is expected to contribute to further volatility on Tuesday.

items.title: RBI draft norms on mis-selling may hit private banks harder
items.description: New RBI draft norms to curb mis-selling of financial products are poised to impact private sector banks more significantly due to their higher reliance on insurance income. These banks have seen a notable increase in insurance income's share of their 'other income' over recent years, a trend less pronounced in public sector banks.

items.title: D-Street’s newest row makes big revenue noise
items.description: Nine of India’s top 12 new-age firms grew revenues by 25% or more in October–December, driven by wider market reach and rising digital demand. Eternal, owner of Zomato and Blinkit, tripled its revenue, while Swiggy, Ather Energy, Lenskart, PB Fintech, Meesho and Nykaa saw 25–54% growth. Profits also rose at Nykaa, Eternal, Delhivery and Paytm.

items.title: What do RBI’s new rules mean for investors in exchange and brokerage stocks?
items.description: The RBI has tightened norms for bank loans to brokers and capital market intermediaries, mandating 100% collateral for credit facilities. These changes, effective April 1, aim to increase transparency and will impact proprietary trading desks significantly by reducing leverage and increasing borrowing costs for brokers.

items.title: Battered IT loses weight on Nifty, banking hits new high
items.description: Information Technology stocks are losing their grip on the Nifty index. Banks are now more influential, their share in the key index reaching new highs. This shift reflects changing earnings trajectories and investor sentiment. Overseas investors have significantly reduced their exposure to Indian IT. The impact of Artificial Intelligence disruption is a key concern for the sector.

items.title: NSE IPO survives High Court hurdle
items.description: The Delhi High Court has dismissed a petition challenging the Securities and Exchange Board of India's approval for the National Stock Exchange's initial public offering. Justice Jasmeet Singh stated the petition aimed to halt the exchange's IPO. The court found no grounds to interfere with Sebi's decision.

items.title: ET Now GBS 2026: AI can cut healthcare costs and bridge talent gap, say industry leaders
items.description: Industry leaders emphasized the need for academia and industry collaboration to equip India's youth for AI-centric jobs. AI is poised to significantly reduce healthcare costs and bridge the talent gap, enabling remote medical expertise and improving diagnostic efficiency.  This technological revolution presents a substantial opportunity for India's demographic dividend.

items.title: Jindal Power, Vedanta and Havells among 22 bidders for Gupta Power
items.description: Jindal Power, Vedanta, and Havells India are among 22 potential bidders for debt-laden Gupta Power Infrastructure, which faces insolvency proceedings with admitted claims of ₹4,240 crore. Other interested parties include UltraTech Cement and Torrent Electricals. The company manufactures cables, wire rods, and conductors.

items.title: Ola Electric shares plunge another 5% to hit all-time low after Citi slashes target price by 51%
items.description: Shares of Ola Electric Mobility hit a fresh 52-week low after Citi downgraded the stock to Sell and cut its target price by 51%. As reported by ET Now, the brokerage flagged weak EV penetration, market-share losses and concerns over cash flows.

items.title: Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions IPO: Price band fixed at Rs 1,000–Rs 1,053; all you need to know
items.description: CleanMax Enviro Energy Solutions is set to launch its Rs 3,100 crore IPO. The subscription period will be from February 23 to February 25. The company plans to raise funds for growth and debt repayment. This move comes as India's renewable energy sector gains momentum. CleanMax is a leading provider of renewable energy solutions for commercial and industrial clients.

items.title: Dividend alert: Last chance to buy Coal India, HAL, and 6 other stocks today. Details here
items.description: Eight stocks are set to trade ex-dividend on February 18, as the companies have fixed this date as the record date to determine shareholders eligible for the dividend. Investors seeking to qualify must purchase these stocks today, prior to the ex-dividend date.

items.title: IT stocks attempt to get back on their feet. What Nomura, UBS and Citi are saying
items.description: Information technology stocks rebounded after a steep two-week selloff, with Infosys, TCS and Wipro rising up to 3%. Investor concerns over AI-driven disruption eased slightly, even as brokerages warned that advances in agentic AI could challenge traditional IT services models. Analysts, however, see scope for business-model evolution and selective opportunities.

items.title: Infosys shares rise 3% on collaboration with Anthropic. Here’s what Salil Parekh said
items.description: Infosys shares rose on Tuesday after the IT major announced a strategic partnership with AI firm Anthropic to build enterprise-ready AI solutions. The collaboration will integrate Claude models with Infosys Topaz to automate workflows, boost software development and scale AI adoption across sectors, lifting investor sentiment and the stock price.

items.title: SBI’s 70% rally narrows valuation gap with HDFC Bank, ICICI — buy, hold or book profits?
items.description: State Bank of India's (SBI) stock has surged nearly 70% in the past year, narrowing its valuation gap with private sector giants like HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank. Experts suggest SBI is now fairly valued, with strong fundamentals and earnings visibility supporting its current price, though further significant upside may be limited.

items.title: Buy, Sell or Hold: Nuvama maintains buy on NBCC India; Citi recommends buy on BEL
items.description: Brokerages see opportunities in defence, pharma, and infrastructure. Nuvama backs NBCC India with a target of Rs 139. Citi remains bullish on Bharat Electronics with a target of Rs 525. Elara Securities reiterates Lupin Limited as Accumulate with a target of Rs 2,447. These recommendations factor in future growth potential.

items.title: Sensex falls over 200 pts, Nifty tests 25,700 as metal, auto stocks lead decline; IT bucks trend
items.description: Indian benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex opened lower on Tuesday, impacted by declines in metal and auto stocks. The IT index, however, showed strength, rising over 0.5% to lead sectoral gains. Major laggards included Eternal, Tata Steel, and Reliance Industries, while Asian Paints and Infosys saw upward movement.

items.title: India Inc earnings recovery playing out as expected. 35 stocks to buy after Q3 results
items.description: India Inc's December quarter earnings season largely met expectations, with brokerages turning constructive on select market pockets. Investors have 35 stocks to consider, highlighted for strong earnings momentum or likely near-term turnarounds. The overall earnings revision trajectory has become more palatable, with upgrades supporting profit growth.

items.title: Embassy Developments shares in focus after entering lifestyle, second-home segment with Alibaug project
items.description: Embassy Developments Ltd. secured MahaRERA approval for its Embassy Serenity project in Alibaug, marking its entry into the premium second-home market. This 7-acre development, featuring 52 residences and extensive green spaces, boasts a GDV of Rs 400 crore. The launch is slated for this quarter, with possession expected by 2030, amid strong company operating momentum.

items.title: Mutual funds slash stakes in 9 of 10 IT stocks but Rs 4 lakh crore still at play
items.description: Mutual funds significantly reduced their holdings in nine out of ten major IT stocks in January, driven by concerns that AI will disrupt the traditional outsourcing model. Despite this selling pressure, the sector still holds substantial value, with managers offloading stakes in giants like Infosys and TCS, while showing some interest in Wipro.

items.title: $56 billion AI scare tests resilience of TCS, Infosys and other Indian IT stocks
items.description: Indian IT stocks, including TCS and Infosys, faced a $56 billion market hit amid global AI-related concerns. The sell-off tested the sector’s resilience as investors reacted to fears of automation-driven disruptions and changing technology dynamics. Despite short-term volatility, analysts say strong fundamentals and long-term growth prospects could help these companies navigate the turbulence and recover over time.

items.title: ​Coal India among 5 stocks flashing bullish signals, indicating possible uptrend​
items.description: On February 16, in the Nifty200 pack, five stocks came to the bullish scanner of "White Marubozu", according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. A White Marubozu is a bullish candlestick pattern in technical analysis. It occurs when the opening price is the lowest for a given period, and the closing price is the highest.Represented by a long white (or green) candlestick with no upper shadow and a small or nonexistent lower shadow, it indicates strong buying pressure throughout the session. Traders interpret it as a signal that buyers were in control from the opening to the closing, often using such patterns for informed decisions about future price movements in financial markets.

items.title: Top Nifty picks! Infosys, Tata Steel among 11 stocks with EPS CAGR potential of up to 106%
items.description: Indian markets appear set for a stronger performance in CY26, especially after underperforming by around 26% in USD terms versus MSCI EM in CY25, according to Motilal Oswal. Across market capitalisation segments, large caps saw 16% YoY earnings growth, in line with the broader market. Here’s the full list of the brokerage’s preferred picks.

items.title: GMR Airports among 4 Nifty200 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.description: On February 16, four stocks from the Nifty200 universe that gained over 3% were highlighted in the RSI Trending Up scan, based on data from StockEdge.com. It is an uptrend signal if the RSI value has crossed above 50 from below. "RSI trending up" means that the Relative Strength Index (RSI) values are increasing, suggesting strengthening momentum in the price of the stock. This can indicate potential upward movement and may be used by traders to identify buying opportunities.

items.title: Texmaco Rail shares jump 10% after bagging two orders worth over Rs 240 crore
items.description: Texmaco Rail & Engineering shares saw a significant jump. The company announced it secured two new orders totaling over Rs 242 crore. One order is from Mumbai Railway Vikas Corporation for signalling systems. Another is from South Western Railway for overhead equipment maintenance. These wins expand the company's railway electrification services portfolio.

items.title: Hindalco, NALCO downgraded to ‘reduce’ by InCred Equities. Here are 3 reasons why
items.description: Hindalco and National Aluminium Company shares fell Tuesday. A brokerage firm downgraded both companies. They cited weak growth prospects for the coming quarters. Aluminium prices are expected to decline. Hindalco's large capital expenditure could impact its balance sheet. NALCO's expansion coincides with softening alumina prices. Earnings for both companies are projected to fall.

items.title: Unique Picks: 7 stocks held by a single MF scheme in January; fall up to 55% in a year
items.description: ETMarkets analysed mutual fund portfolios to identify stocks held exclusively by a single mutual fund scheme. In the initial screening for January 2025, 194 such stocks were found. The list was narrowed down to stocks with equity mutual fund holdings exceeding Rs 10 crore as of December 2025, resulting in a final shortlist of 22 stocks.Over the past year, most of these stocks delivered negative returns, with seven falling 25–55%. In contrast, four stocks gained 25–125% and are each held exclusively by a single mutual fund scheme. (Data Source: ACE MF, ACE Equity)

items.title: Time to start thinking beyond 2026? 5 mid-cap stocks from different sectors with an upside potential up to 35%
items.description: As a set of stocks, mid-caps have done poorly in the last year. Some of this was, of course, due to business conditions. Others did poorly because of valuation readjustments. Some stocks were seeing readjustment for quite some time, and the bears just made sure it continued for longer. So, is the valuation adjustment over? When investing in mid-caps, investors need to be clear about three things. One is valuation. Two is the real business of the company. And three is the management. You can err on valuations, but not the other two. They are the key to investing, especially in the mid-cap space.

items.title: Groww, Dixon Tech among 10 stocks that Motilal Oswal is bullish on. Do you own any?
items.description: Indian markets could see improved performance in CY26 after underperforming MSCI EM by around 26% in USD terms in CY25, according to Motilal Oswal. In Q3, largecaps reported 16% year-on-year (YoY) earnings growth, broadly in line with the overall universe, while midcaps grew 15% versus a 22% estimate and smallcaps posted 29% against a 34% forecast. The brokerage has identified 10 preferred stock ideas from the non-Nifty space. Here’s the full list.

items.title: Every second smallcap stock trading 40% below all-time high levels. Right time to buy for long term?
items.description: Nearly half of India’s smallcap stocks trade over forty percent below peaks after a sharp correction, says Abakkus Mutual Fund. Despite volatility, smallcaps have outperformed Nifty 50 long term, offering attractive accumulation opportunities.

items.title: Kwality Wall shares slide 5% after tepid Monday debut; plunges 30% in two sessions
items.description: Kwality Wall’s shares fell sharply on Tuesday, extending their post-listing slide to 30% after a weak debut. The stock faced heavy selling despite LIC acquiring a 6.62% stake. The demerged ice-cream business from HUL also saw an open offer launched at Rs 21.33 per share by a Dutch promoter entity.

items.title: Adani stocks rise up to 3% after group announces $100 billion AI-energy investment plan
items.description: Adani Group stocks rose sharply after the conglomerate announced a $100 billion plan to build renewable-powered, AI-ready data centres by 2035. The initiative includes a 5 GW data centre platform and ties with global tech giants, positioning India as a major AI infrastructure hub.

items.title: Market Wrap: Sensex ends 174 pts higher, Nifty extends gain for second session; IT, PSU bank stocks shine
items.description: Benchmark indices reversed early losses to end higher on Tuesday, supported by gains in IT and PSU bank stocks. The Sensex rose 174 points, and the Nifty closed above 25,700, while broader markets also advanced. Analysts said the index holds strong support at 25,600, with momentum likely to sustain.

items.title: Commodity Radar: Copper prices fall 20% from peak. Is it a sign of a shift in fundamentals?
items.description: Copper prices have seen a significant drop from their peak. Experts believe this is a temporary consolidation phase. Global demand concerns and Lunar New Year holidays in China are impacting prices. However, long-term fundamentals remain strong. Traders are advised to watch for stabilization before entering new positions.

items.title: Infosys-Anthropic deal sparks fresh debate: Is AI now an opportunity, not a threat, for Indian IT?
items.description: Infosys shares jumped up to 5% after announcing a strategic AI collaboration with Anthropic, easing fears that next-gen AI could disrupt Indian IT. The partnership blends Claude models with Infosys Topaz to build agentic AI solutions for regulated industries, shifting the narrative from AI threat to AI-driven opportunity.

items.title: Stock Radar: Adani Ports slips after February 52-week high, time to book profits or buy the dip?
items.description: Adani Ports & Special Economic Zone Ltd hit a fresh 52-week high in February 2026, with chart patterns suggesting an upward trend. Experts recommend buying the stock for a target of Rs 1900 in the next 8-10 months, with a stop loss around Rs 1300.

items.title: Engineers India board to meet on February 26 to consider dividend. Check record date, dividend yield
items.description: Engineers India Limited posts over threefold Q3 net profit jump, driven by turnkey project revenue. Board to meet for second interim dividend, with record date set for March. Shares remain volatile amid profit booking.

items.title: S45 introduces AI-based platform for IPO execution
items.description: India’s capital markets are evolving as S45 launches the nation’s first AI-enabled investment banking platform. It streamlines IPO workflows, improves transparency, accelerates listing processes, and combines AI with human expertise to optimize execution.

items.title: SBI, L&T among 4 stocks that hit 52-week highs & rallied up to 16% in a month
items.description: On Tuesday, the benchmark Sensex climbed about 174 points to finish at 83,450. Amid the broader market rally, four stocks from the BSE 100 index hit their 52-week highs, reflecting their best performance over the past year. Investors generally see such breakouts as bullish indicators, signaling strong upward momentum and potential for further upside.

items.title: Turnaround Story: 7 stocks shift from loss to profit in December '25 quarter
items.description: A "Turnaround Story YoY" indicates that the company has gone from incurring a net loss in the same quarter last year to reporting a net profit in the latest quarter, signaling a significant improvement in its financial performance and recovery. In the NSE list of stocks with a market cap over Rs 10,000 crore, we have highlighted the top 7 stocks that reported net profits in the December '25 quarter, compared to net losses in the same quarter last year, according to StockEdge profitability scan data.

items.title: Fractal Analytics rebounds from weak debut; shares jump 4% as brokerage initiates coverage with 40% upside call
items.description: Fractal Analytics rebounded after a weak market debut, rising over 9% intraday after Prabhudas Lilladher initiated coverage with a 40% upside target. The brokerage cited strong account mining, steady growth across tiers, deep AI capabilities, and improving prospects for Fractal Alpha as key drivers supporting long-term momentum.

items.title: ​Gainers & Losers: Fractal Analytics, Infosys among 6 stocks in limelight on Tuesday​
Market Trading Guide: LG Electronics, Bank of India among 3 stocks to buy on Wednesday for up to 9% upside
items.description: Nifty ended with gains on Tuesday, recording its second successive positive closing, aided by IT, consumer and financial stocks even as energy and metals dragged markets. The index has continued its recovery but is facing resistance near the 50DMA on the higher side Rupak De, Senior Technical Analyst at LKP Securities expects Nifty to remain rangebound or witness a minor pullback, given the rejection at this critical moving average. "The RSI has also entered a bearish crossover, indicating weakening momentum. On the upside, resistance is placed at 25,800, above which the index could witness further strength. However, failure to move past 25,800 may trigger long unwinding, potentially dragging the index back toward the 25,500 level," De said.Here are 3 stock recommendations for Wednesday:

items.title: Two Trades for Today: 2 PSU banking stocks, one a large-cap and the other a mid-cap, both for about 5% upmove
items.description: Our technical analysts identify select stocks that may gain momentum even in volatile markets. Here are the technical calls for today.

items.title: Carlsberg considering listing of India business to unlock shareholders value: Group CEO
items.description: Danish brewing giant Carlsberg is considering listing its India business, as it looks to unlock shareholder value in the country where it is witnessing strong growth, said its Group CEO Jacob Aarup-Andersen.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Wednesday
items.description: Indian benchmarks extended gains for a second session, led by IT, consumer and financial stocks. Nifty faced resistance near its 50DMA, while easing volatility supported sentiment. Analysts advise buy-on-dips amid rangebound movement and selective stock activity.

items.title: Stock Radar: Astral stock hits fresh 52-week highs; what should traders do – buy or book profits?
items.description: Astral Ltd, a plastic products company, reached a fresh 52-week high, breaking out of an ascending triangle pattern. Experts suggest buying opportunities for short-term traders targeting Rs 1,772-1,800 in the next 3-4 weeks, with a stop loss below Rs 1,460.
